from manim import *
import numpy as np

class Concept02LogisticRegressionFeatures(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Coordinate System for Sigmoid Curve
        # The prompt asks for a NumberPlane on the right
        plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-6, 6, 1],
            y_range=[-0.1, 1.1, 0.5],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.2}
        ).shift(RIGHT * 2.5)
        
        # Sigmoid logic using a named function to avoid lambda-related identifier issues
        def calculate_sigmoid(z_input):
            return 1 / (1 + np.exp(-z_input))
            
        sigmoid_curve = plane.plot(calculate_sigmoid, color=GREEN)
        curve_label = MathTex("P(y=1)", color=GREEN).next_to(plane, UP, buff=0.2)
        
        # 2. Trackers for Interactive Features
        val_1day = ValueTracker(0)
        val_30day = ValueTracker(0)
        
        # 3. Sliding Bars (UI on the left)
        bar_w = 0.5
        bar_h = 3
        
        # 1-Day T-CSP Bar
        frame_1day = Rectangle(width=bar_w, height=bar_h, stroke_color=WHITE).shift(LEFT * 5 + DOWN * 0.5)
        label_1day = Text("1-Day T-CSP", font_size=16).next_to(frame_1day, DOWN)
        
        def update_fill_1day():
            curr_val = val_1day.get_value()
            # Map range [-3, 3] to bar height [0.01, bar_h]
            mapped_h = np.clip((curr_val + 3) / 6 * bar_h, 0.01, bar_h)
            return Rectangle(
                width=bar_w, height=mapped_h, 
                fill_color=RED, fill_opacity=0.7, stroke_width=0
            ).align_to(frame_1day, DOWN)
            
        fill_1day = always_redraw(update_fill_1day)
        
        # 30-Day T-CSP Bar
        frame_30day = Rectangle(width=bar_w, height=bar_h, stroke_color=WHITE).shift(LEFT * 3.5 + DOWN * 0.5)
        label_30day = Text("30-Day T-CSP", font_size=16).next_to(frame_30day, DOWN)
        
        def update_fill_30day():
            curr_val = val_30day.get_value()
            mapped_h = np.clip((curr_val + 3) / 6 * bar_h, 0.01, bar_h)
            return Rectangle(
                width=bar_w, height=mapped_h, 
                fill_color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.7, stroke_width=0
            ).align_to(frame_30day, DOWN)
            
        fill_30day = always_redraw(update_fill_30day)
        
        # 4. Formulas and Mapping
        # Feature Vector X
        feature_vector = MathTex(
            r"\mathbf{X} = [1, X_{1d}, X_{30d}]",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5).shift(LEFT * 3.5)
        
        # Logistic Formula
        logistic_formula = MathTex(
            r"P(y=1) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{1d} + \beta_{30} X_{30d})}}",
            font_size=30
        ).next_to(feature_vector, DOWN, buff=0.7)
        
        # Moving Dot on the Curve
        dot_on_curve = Dot(color=YELLOW, radius=0.1)
        
        def move_dot_on_curve(mobject_to_update):
            # Sum features to determine position along sigmoid
            total_z = val_1day.get_value() + val_30day.get_value()
            current_prob = calculate_sigmoid(total_z)
            mobject_to_update.move_to(plane.c2p(total_z, current_prob))
            
        dot_on_curve.add_updater(move_dot_on_curve)
        
        # 5. Animation Sequence
        self.add(plane, sigmoid_curve, curve_label)
        self.add(frame_1day, label_1day, fill_1day, frame_30day, label_30day, fill_30day)
        
        self.play(Write(feature_vector))
        self.wait(0.5)
        # Using ReplacementTransform as a robust alternative for formula mapping
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(feature_vector.copy(), logistic_formula))
        self.add(dot_on_curve)
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Animate the bars sliding and the dot moving along the curve
        self.play(
            val_1day.animate.set_value(2),
            val_30day.animate.set_value(1.5),
            run_time=3,
            rate_func=rate_functions.linear
        )
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        self.play(
            val_1day.animate.set_value(-2.5),
            val_30day.animate.set_value(-1.5),
            run_time=4,
            rate_func=rate_functions.smooth
        )
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class TheElbowThreshold(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        axes_obj = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)

        def get_curve_y(recall_input):
            return 1 - (1 - recall_input)**4

        curve_graph = axes_obj.plot(get_curve_y, x_range=[0, 1], color=BLUE)

        axis_label_x = Text("Fraction of Zones Flagged", font_size=20)
        axis_label_y = Text("Recall", font_size=20)
        labels_obj = axes_obj.get_axis_labels(x_label=axis_label_x, y_label=axis_label_y)

        zones_container = VGroup()
        for placeholder_idx in range(20):
            zones_container.add(Square(side_length=0.4, fill_opacity=0.2, color=GRAY, stroke_width=2))
        zones_container.arrange_in_grid(rows=4, cols=5, buff=0.1)
        zones_container.to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1)

        val_tracker = ValueTracker(0.05)
        moving_dot_obj = Dot(color=YELLOW)

        def update_dot_position(mobject_param):
            current_value_x = val_tracker.get_value()
            current_value_y = get_curve_y(current_value_x)
            mobject_param.move_to(axes_obj.c2p(current_value_x, current_value_y))

        moving_dot_obj.add_updater(update_dot_position)

        def update_zones_color_state(vgroup_param):
            highlight_threshold = int(val_tracker.get_value() * 20)
            for loop_idx, square_mobj in enumerate(vgroup_param):
                if loop_idx < highlight_threshold:
                    square_mobj.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0.8)
                    square_mobj.set_color(YELLOW)
                else:
                    square_mobj.set_fill(GRAY, opacity=0.2)
                    square_mobj.set_color(GRAY)

        zones_container.add_updater(update_zones_color_state)

        formula_tex_obj = MathTex(r"Recall = \frac{TP}{TP + FN}").scale(0.8).to_corner(UL)
        
        def linear_rate_func(t_param):
            return t_param

        self.add(axes_obj, labels_obj, zones_container, formula_tex_obj)
        self.play(Create(curve_graph), run_time=2)
        self.add(moving_dot_obj)
        
        self.play(
            val_tracker.animate.set_value(0.9), 
            run_time=4, 
            rate_func=linear_rate_func
        )
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(val_tracker.animate.set_value(0.35), run_time=2)
        
        elbow_point_coord = axes_obj.c2p(0.35, get_curve_y(0.35))
        elbow_indicator_circle = Circle(radius=0.4, color=YELLOW).move_to(elbow_point_coord)
        elbow_text_label = Text("The Elbow", font_size=24, color=YELLOW).next_to(elbow_indicator_circle, UR)
        
        self.play(Create(elbow_indicator_circle), Write(elbow_text_label))
        self.play(Flash(elbow_point_coord, color=YELLOW))
        self.play(Indicate(elbow_indicator_circle))
        
        self.wait(3)

{
  "id": "concept-02-zero-shot-classification",
  "voice": "Once the model is trained, we can use it as a zero-shot classifier. For a new image, we generate its vector representation, v. We then compare this against a pre-computed matrix, X, which contains the text embeddings for every possible country. By taking the dot product and applying a softmax function, we get a probability distribution. The country with the highest score is our first-stage prediction. It's like asking the model, 'Which of these 140 country descriptions does this visual scene look like most?'",
  "goal": "Understand the mathematical mechanism of using CLIP as a linear classifier for country prediction.",
  "remarks": "Show a vector 'v' representing the image. Next to it, show a large matrix 'X' where each row is a country name. Use a pulsing highlight to show the dot product operation between 'v' and each row of 'X'. Create a bar chart that updates in real-time to show the results of the Softmax function, with one bar (e.g., France) shooting up while others drop. Use MathTex to transform the dot product expression into the final probability vector.",
  "formula": "h(\\mathbf{v};\\mathbf{X})=\\frac{\\exp(\\mathbf{X}\\mathbf{v})}{\\sum_{j=1}^{N}\\exp(\\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\\mathbf{v})}",
  "image_ref": "img-8.jpeg"
}
```

Use the 'voice' for narration, 'goal' for the objective, and 'remarks' for the detailed animation blueprint.

Also, use this formula as a reference:
h(\mathbf{v};\mathbf{X})=\frac{\exp(\mathbf{X}\mathbf{v})}{\sum_{j=1}^{N}\exp(\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\mathbf{v})}
generated_code:
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ZeroShotClassification(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Formula reference at the top
        formula = MathTex(
            r"h(\mathbf{v};\mathbf{X})=\frac{\exp(\mathbf{X}\mathbf{v})}{\sum_{j=1}^{N}\exp(\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\mathbf{v})}",
            font_size=36
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)

        # 2. Image Representation (Vector v)
        image_box = Rectangle(height=2.2, width=2.2, color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.3)
        image_text = Text("Image", font_size=18).move_to(image_box.get_center())
        image_group = VGroup(image_box, image_text).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1).shift(UP * 0.5)
        
        v_vector = Matrix([[0.8], [0.2], [0.5], ["\\vdots"]]).scale(0.5).next_to(image_group, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        v_label = MathTex(r"\mathbf{v}").next_to(v_vector, UP, buff=0.1)
        
        # 3. Matrix X (Text embeddings for countries)
        countries = ["USA", "France", "Japan", "Brazil", "India", "Egypt"]
        matrix_rows = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                Text(c, font_size=16).set_width(0.8, stretch=True),
                Rectangle(height=0.25, width=1.5, color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.4)
            ).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.1)
            for c in countries
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.15).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1).shift(UP * 0.5)
        
        x_label = MathTex(r"\mathbf{X}").next_to(matrix_rows, UP, buff=0.1)
        x_bracket = SurroundingRectangle(matrix_rows, color=WHITE, buff=0.1)
        matrix_X = VGroup(matrix_rows, x_label, x_bracket)

        # 4. Bar Chart for Softmax (Probability Distribution)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 6, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1, 0.5],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=2,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        
        bar_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(c, font_size=14).next_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, 0), DOWN, buff=0.1)
            for i, c in enumerate(countries)
        ])
        
        bars = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(
                width=0.5, 
                height=0.01, 
                color=BLUE, 
                fill_opacity=0.7
            ).move_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN)
            for i in range(len(countries))
        ])

        # 5. Initialization
        self.add(formula)
        self.play(FadeIn(image_group), Create(v_vector), Write(v_label))
        self.play(Create(matrix_X))
        self.play(Create(axes), Create(bar_labels))
        self.add(bars)

        # 6. Animation: Dot Product and Softmax Update
        highlight = Rectangle(
            height=0.35, width=2.6, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=4
        ).move_to(matrix_rows[0])
        
        # Probabilities: France (index 1) gets the highest score
        probs = [0.1, 0.8, 0.05, 0.02, 0.02, 0.01]
        
        for i in range(len(countries)):
            # Move highlight to current row in X
            self.play(highlight.animate.move_to(matrix_rows[i]), run_time=0.4)
            
            # Show connection line for dot product
            dot_line = Line(v_vector.get_right(), matrix_rows[i].get_left(), color=YELLOW, stroke_width=2)
            self.play(Create(dot_line), run_time=0.1)
            
            # Update bar chart height based on probability
            target_height = probs[i] * 1.8 # Scaled for visual clarity
            self.play(
                bars[i].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(max(0.05, target_height)).move_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN),
                FadeOut(dot_line),
                run_time=0.3
            )
            
            if i == 1: # France highlight
                self.play(Indicate(matrix_rows[i], color=GOLD), bars[i].animate.set_color(GREEN))
                
        # Final mathematical summary
        prob_vec_label = MathTex(r"\text{Softmax}(\mathbf{X}\mathbf{v})", font_size=32).next_to(axes, UP, buff=0.2)
        self.play(Write(prob_vec_label))
        self.play(FadeOut(highlight))
        
        self.wait(2)

import numpy as np

class ZeroShotClassification(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        voice_text = "Once the model is trained, we can use it as a zero-shot classifier..."
        
        # 1. Setup Image Placeholder
        image_rect = Rectangle(height=3, width=4, color=GRAY).shift(LEFT * 4 + UP * 1.5)
        image_label = Text("Input Image", font_size=24).move_to(image_rect)
        image_group = VGroup(image_rect, image_label)
        
        # 2. Manual construction of Vector v (instead of using Matrix class)
        v_elements = VGroup(
            MathTex("0.1"), MathTex("0.8"), MathTex("0.3"), MathTex("\\vdots"), MathTex("0.5")
        ).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.2)
        v_bracket_l = MathTex("[").scale(2.5).next_to(v_elements, LEFT, buff=0.1)
        v_bracket_r = MathTex("]").scale(2.5).next_to(v_elements, RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        v_vector = VGroup(v_bracket_l, v_elements, v_bracket_r).scale(0.7).next_to(image_rect, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        v_label = MathTex("\\mathbf{v}").next_to(v_vector, UP)

        self.play(FadeIn(image_group))
        self.play(Create(v_vector), Write(v_label))

        # 3. Manual construction of Matrix X (Country Names)
        countries = ["France", "Japan", "USA", "Brazil", "China"]
        x_elements = VGroup(*[Text(c, font_size=20) for c in countries])
        x_elements.add(MathTex("\\vdots", font_size=24))
        x_elements.add(Text("Norway", font_size=20))
        x_elements.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.25)
        
        x_bracket_l = MathTex("[").scale(4).next_to(x_elements, LEFT, buff=0.1)
        x_bracket_r = MathTex("]").scale(4).next_to(x_elements, RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        matrix_X = VGroup(x_bracket_l, x_elements, x_bracket_r).scale(0.8).next_to(v_vector, RIGHT, buff=1)
        X_label = MathTex("\\mathbf{X}").next_to(matrix_X, UP)
        
        self.play(Create(matrix_X), Write(X_label))

        # 4. Highlight Dot Product
        # Highlight first row of X and vector v
        highlight_row = SurroundingRectangle(x_elements[0], color=YELLOW, buff=0.1)
        highlight_v = SurroundingRectangle(v_elements, color=YELLOW, buff=0.1)
        
        dot_expr = MathTex("\\mathbf{X}\\mathbf{v}").to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.2)
        
        self.play(Write(dot_expr))
        for _ in range(2):
            self.play(Create(highlight_row), Create(highlight_v), run_time=0.4)
            self.play(FadeOut(highlight_row), FadeOut(highlight_v), run_time=0.4)

        # 5. Bar Chart for Softmax
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 6, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1, 0.5],
            x_length=4,
            y_length=2.5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5).shift(DOWN * 1.2)
        
        chart_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(countries[i], font_size=14).next_to(axes.c2p(i+1, 0), DOWN)
            for i in range(len(countries))
        ])

        # Create bars as individual Rectangles
        bars = VGroup()
        for i in range(len(countries)):
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.4, 
                height=0.1, 
                fill_opacity=0.8, 
                fill_color=BLUE
            ).move_to(axes.c2p(i+1, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN)
            bars.add(bar)

        self.play(Create(axes), Write(chart_labels), Create(bars))

        # 6. Animation: Bars update and formula transforms
        target_heights = [0.8, 0.1, 0.15, 0.05, 0.1]
        bar_animations = []
        for i, bar in enumerate(bars):
            # Scale rectangle height relative to axes
            new_height = target_heights[i] * (axes.c2p(0, 1)[1] - axes.c2p(0, 0)[1])
            new_bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.4, 
                height=max(new_height, 0.01), 
                fill_opacity=0.8, 
                fill_color=RED if i == 0 else BLUE
            ).move_to(axes.c2p(i+1, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN)
            bar_animations.append(Transform(bar, new_bar))

        softmax_formula = MathTex(
            "h(\\mathbf{v};\\mathbf{X}) = \\frac{\\exp(\\mathbf{X}\\mathbf{v})}{\\sum_{j=1}^{N}\\exp(\\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\\mathbf{v})}"
        ).scale(0.75).move_to(dot_expr)

        self.play(
            *bar_animations,
            Transform(dot_expr, softmax_formula),
            run_time=2
        )

        # 7. Conclusion Label
        pred_text = Text("Prediction: France", font_size=24, color=RED).next_to(bars[0], UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(pred_text))
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptGridEncoding(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Hexagonal Board Setup (Radius 1)
        # Formula: n = 4 * radius + 1 = 5
        board_radius = 1
        n = 5
        hex_board = VGroup()
        # Axial coordinate system for hexagonal grid
        for q in range(-board_radius, board_radius + 1):
            r1 = max(-board_radius, -q - board_radius)
            r2 = min(board_radius, -q + board_radius)
            for r in range(r1, r2 + 1):
                # Hex to 2D Cartesian
                x = 0.7 * (3/2 * q)
                y = 0.7 * (np.sqrt(3)/2 * q + np.sqrt(3) * r)
                circle = Circle(radius=0.25, color=WHITE, stroke_width=3)
                circle.move_to([x, y, 0])
                hex_board.add(circle)
        
        self.play(Create(hex_board))
        self.wait(1)

        # 2. Simulate tilting into 3D Perspective (using 2D transformation)
        # Shear and scale to give perspective illusion
        tilted_matrix = [[1, 0.5], [0, 0.5]]
        self.play(
            hex_board.animate.apply_matrix(tilted_matrix).scale(0.8).shift(DOWN * 1.5),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Create three square grid layers (5x5)
        def create_layer(color, title_text, highlight_indices):
            layer_group = VGroup()
            grid = VGroup()
            for i in range(n * n):
                rect = Square(side_length=0.4, stroke_width=1, stroke_color=WHITE)
                if i in highlight_indices:
                    rect.set_fill(color, opacity=0.7)
                else:
                    rect.set_fill(color, opacity=0.1)
                grid.add(rect)
            grid.arrange_in_grid(rows=n, cols=n, buff=0.05)
            
            # Apply same "perspective" matrix to the square grid
            grid.apply_matrix(tilted_matrix)
            
            label = Text(title_text, font_size=18, color=color)
            label.next_to(grid, LEFT, buff=0.2)
            
            layer_group.add(grid, label)
            return layer_group

        # Sample indices for "pieces"
        layer1 = create_layer(BLUE, "Current Player", [6, 7, 12])
        layer2 = create_layer(GREEN, "Target Zone", [17, 18, 22, 23])
        layer3 = create_layer(RED, "Opponents", [2, 3, 8])

        # Position layers in a 2D stack to simulate vertical 3D floating
        layer1.move_to(ORIGIN + DOWN * 1.0)
        layer2.move_to(layer1.get_center() + UP * 1.2 + RIGHT * 0.4)
        layer3.move_to(layer2.get_center() + UP * 1.2 + RIGHT * 0.4)

        # 4. Transform hex board into the first square layer
        # Using first grid (layer1[0]) for the transform
        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(hex_board, layer1[0]),
            FadeIn(layer1[1])
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 5. Animate stacking of other layers
        self.play(
            FadeIn(layer2, shift=UP * 0.5 + RIGHT * 0.2),
            run_time=1
        )
        self.play(
            FadeIn(layer3, shift=UP * 0.5 + RIGHT * 0.2),
            run_time=1
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Add labels and formula
        formula = MathTex("n = 4 \\times \\text{board radius} + 1", font_size=32)
        formula.to_corner(UL)
        
        dimensions = MathTex("n \\times n \\times 3", font_size=44, color=YELLOW)
        dimensions.to_corner(UR, buff=1)
        
        explanation = Text(
            "Multi-layered Square Grid Encoding", 
            font_size=24, 
            slant=ITALIC
        ).to_edge(BOTTOM, buff=0.5)

        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(Write(dimensions))
        self.play(FadeIn(explanation))
        
        # Subtle "float" animation for the stack
        self.play(
            VGroup(layer1, layer2, layer3).animate.shift(UP * 0.2),
            rate_func=there_and_back,
            run_time=2
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

generated_code:
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptGridEncoding(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Parameters
        board_radius = 2
        n = 4 * board_radius + 1  # n = 9
        
        # 2. Create Hexagonal Grid
        hex_grid = VGroup()
        for r in range(-board_radius * 2, board_radius * 2 + 1):
            for q in range(-board_radius * 2, board_radius * 2 + 1):
                if abs(r + q) <= board_radius * 2:
                    # Axial to Pixel conversion
                    x = 0.4 * (1.5 * q)
                    y = 0.4 * (np.sqrt(3)/2 * q + np.sqrt(3) * r)
                    hex_grid.add(Circle(radius=0.1, color=WHITE, fill_opacity=0.2, fill_color=WHITE).move_to([x, y, 0]))
        
        hex_grid.move_to(ORIGIN)

        # Narration 1
        intro_text = Text(
            "Transforming the hexagonal board into grids.",
            font_size=24
        ).to_edge(UP)
        
        self.play(Create(hex_grid), Write(intro_text))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Simulate 3D Tilt (Pseudo-3D using 2D transforms)
        # We squash vertically and shear to give a perspective feel
        tilt_group = VGroup(hex_grid)
        self.play(
            tilt_group.animate.apply_matrix([[1, 0.5, 0], [0, 0.5, 0], [0, 0, 1]]),
            intro_text.animate.to_corner(UL).scale(0.7),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. Morph into a Square Grid
        # Create a grid of squares (n x n)
        side = 0.3
        square_grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=side).move_to([i * (side + 0.05) - (n * side)/2, j * (side + 0.05) - (n * side)/2, 0])
            for i in range(n)
            for j in range(n)
        ]).set_stroke(WHITE, 1)
        
        # Apply the same "tilt" to the square grid so the transition is seamless
        square_grid.apply_matrix([[1, 0.5, 0], [0, 0.5, 0], [0, 0, 1]])

        self.play(ReplacementTransform(hex_grid, square_grid), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 5. Split into Three Layers
        # We'll create 3 colored copies and offset them vertically in the 2D plane
        # to look like a stack.
        layer_spacing = 1.2
        
        layer_self = square_grid.copy().set_color(BLUE).set_stroke(BLUE, 1)
        layer_target = square_grid.copy().set_color(GREEN).set_stroke(GREEN, 1)
        layer_opponent = square_grid.copy().set_color(RED).set_stroke(RED, 1)
        
        # Add semi-transparency and some "occupied" cells
        layers = [layer_self, layer_target, layer_opponent]
        colors = [BLUE, GREEN, RED]
        
        for layer, color in zip(layers, colors):
            layer.set_fill(color, opacity=0.3)
            # Highlight some cells to represent pieces
            for k in range(0, len(layer), 10):
                layer[k].set_fill(color, opacity=0.9)

        # Position them in a vertical stack
        stack = VGroup(
            layer_self.shift(UP * layer_spacing),
            layer_target,
            layer_opponent.shift(DOWN * layer_spacing)
        ).move_to(LEFT * 2)

        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(VGroup(square_grid), stack),
            run_time=2
        )

        # 6. Labels and Formula
        label_dim = MathTex("n \\times n \\times 3").scale(1.2).next_to(stack, RIGHT, buff=1)
        label_formula = MathTex("n = 4 \\times \\text{board radius} + 1").scale(0.7).to_corner(DL)
        
        layer_labels = VGroup(
            Text("Layer 1: Self", font_size=18, color=BLUE).next_to(layer_self, RIGHT),
            Text("Layer 2: Target", font_size=18, color=GREEN).next_to(layer_target, RIGHT),
            Text("Layer 3: Opponent", font_size=18, color=RED).next_to(layer_opponent, RIGHT)
        )

        voiceover_text2 = Text(
            "The CNN 'sees' spatial relationships between pegs and goals.",
            font_size=20
        ).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(
            Write(label_dim),
            Write(label_formula),
            Write(layer_labels),
            FadeIn(voiceover_text2)
        )
        
        self.wait(3)

  "formula": "P_{t+1} = \\arg\\max_{c \\in \\text{Children}(P_t)} \\text{WinRate}(c)",
  "image_ref": "img-1.jpeg"
}

from manim import *

class GeneticWinRateSelection(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Formula and Title
        formula = MathTex(r"P_{t+1} = \arg\max_{c \in \text{Children}(P_t)} \text{WinRate}(c)")
        formula.to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        # 2. Central Parent Node
        parent = VGroup(
            Circle(radius=0.7, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.2),
            Text("Parent P_t", font_size=24)
        ).shift(UP * 1.0)
        
        self.play(Create(parent))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Three Child Nodes Branching Out
        child_targets = [LEFT * 4 + DOWN * 1, DOWN * 1, RIGHT * 4 + DOWN * 1]
        children = VGroup()
        arrows = VGroup()

        for i, pos in enumerate(child_targets):
            child = VGroup(
                Circle(radius=0.6, color=WHITE, fill_opacity=0.1),
                Text(f"Child {i+1}", font_size=20)
            ).move_to(pos)
            arrow = Arrow(parent.get_bottom(), child.get_top(), buff=0.1)
            children.add(child)
            arrows.add(arrow)

        self.play(
            LaggedStart(*[Create(a) for a in arrows], lag_ratio=0.2),
            LaggedStart(*[Create(c) for c in children], lag_ratio=0.2)
        )

        # 4. Bar Charts (Win Rates) represented by Rectangles
        # We define heights for the bars
        win_rate_values = [0.3, 0.9, 0.5] # Child 2 is the winner
        bars = VGroup()
        bar_labels = VGroup()
        
        for i, (child, val) in enumerate(zip(children, win_rate_values)):
            # Create a base for the bar
            base_line = Line(LEFT*0.5, RIGHT*0.5).next_to(child, DOWN, buff=0.3)
            # Create the bar itself
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.6, 
                height=0.1, # Start small
                fill_opacity=0.8, 
                color=BLUE_C
            ).move_to(base_line.get_center(), aligned_edge=DOWN)
            
            label = Text("Win Rate", font_size=16).next_to(base_line, DOWN, buff=0.1)
            
            bars.add(bar)
            bar_labels.add(VGroup(base_line, label))

        self.play(FadeIn(bar_labels))
        
        # 5. Animate bars growing to their specific heights
        growth_animations = []
        for bar, val in zip(bars, win_rate_values):
            growth_animations.append(bar.animate.stretch_to_fit_height(val * 2.0, about_edge=DOWN))
        
        self.play(*growth_animations, run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Highlight the child with the highest bar in gold
        winner_idx = 1
        winner_child = children[winner_idx]
        winner_bar = bars[winner_idx]
        
        highlight_rect = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(winner_child, winner_bar), color=GOLD, buff=0.2)
        
        self.play(
            Create(highlight_rect),
            winner_bar.animate.set_color(GOLD),
            winner_child[0].animate.set_color(GOLD)
        )
        self.play(Indicate(winner_child))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Fade out parent and other children
        losers = VGroup(
            parent,
            arrows,
            children[0], bars[0], bar_labels[0],
            children[2], bars[2], bar_labels[2],
            bar_labels[1], # the label for the winner bar
            highlight_rect
        )
        
        self.play(FadeOut(losers))

        # 8. Move the gold child to center to become the new parent
        new_parent_label = Text("New Parent P_{t+1}", font_size=30, color=GOLD)
        
        self.play(
            winner_child.animate.move_to(UP * 1.0),
            FadeOut(winner_bar)
        )
        
        # Replace child text with "New Parent"
        self.play(Transform(winner_child[1], new_parent_label.move_to(winner_child[1].get_center())))
        
        # 9. Final text to close out the "Evolution" loop
        evolution_text = Text("Evolutionary Gradient for Victory", font_size=32, color=GOLD).shift(DOWN * 2)
        self.play(Write(evolution_text))
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup - 2D plane of dots
        num_dots = 40
        dots = VGroup()
        for _ in range(num_dots):
            dot = Dot(
                point=[np.random.uniform(-5, 5), np.random.uniform(-3, 2), 0],
                radius=0.08,
                color=PURPLE
            )
            dots.add(dot)
        
        # Define beta values
        betas = np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.5, num_dots)
        # Select 3 condensation points with high beta
        high_beta_indices = [5, 15, 25]
        for idx in high_beta_indices:
            betas[idx] = 0.95

        # 2. Formula Label
        formula = MathTex(r"q_i = \text{arctanh}(\beta_i) + q_{min}")
        formula.to_edge(UP)
        
        self.add(dots)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Animate growth and color change based on beta
        # We'll use a simple color palette to simulate interpolation
        color_palette = [PURPLE, BLUE, TEAL, GREEN, YELLOW]
        
        grow_anims = []
        for i, dot in enumerate(dots):
            beta = betas[i]
            # Manual color selection based on beta
            color_idx = int(beta * (len(color_palette) - 1))
            target_color = color_palette[color_idx]
            # Manual radius interpolation
            target_radius = 0.05 * (1 - beta) + 0.25 * beta
            
            grow_anims.append(
                dot.animate.set_color(target_color).scale(target_radius / 0.08)
            )
            
        self.play(*grow_anims, run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Create "Gravity Wells" (Concentric circles with gradient feel)
        wells = VGroup()
        condensation_points = []
        for idx in high_beta_indices:
            cp = dots[idx]
            condensation_points.append(cp)
            
            # Create a localized "mesh" or gradient well
            well = VGroup()
            for r in np.linspace(0.2, 1.8, 8):
                ring = Circle(radius=r, stroke_width=1, stroke_opacity=0.4 - r*0.2)
                ring.set_color(YELLOW)
                ring.move_to(cp.get_center())
                well.add(ring)
            wells.add(well)

        self.play(FadeIn(wells, lag_ratio=0.1))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Pulling effect - dots slide toward high-beta centers
        pull_anims = []
        for i, dot in enumerate(dots):
            if i in high_beta_indices:
                continue
            
            # Find the closest condensation point
            dot_pos = dot.get_center()
            distances = [np.linalg.norm(dot_pos - cp.get_center()) for cp in condensation_points]
            nearest_idx = np.argmin(distances)
            target_cp = condensation_points[nearest_idx]
            
            # Only pull if within range (to simulate gravity)
            if distances[nearest_idx] < 4.0:
                # Move 80% of the way to the center
                destination = dot_pos + 0.8 * (target_cp.get_center() - dot_pos)
                pull_anims.append(dot.animate.move_to(destination))

        # Using 'rush_into' as a standard Manim rate function for acceleration
        self.play(*pull_anims, run_time=2, rate_func=rush_into)
        self.wait(2)

        # Clean up
        self.play(
            FadeOut(dots),
            FadeOut(wells),
            FadeOut(formula)
        )
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *
import numpy as np
import random

class ObjectCondensation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Plane and Formula
        plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-7, 7, 1],
            y_range=[-4, 4, 1],
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.2}
        )
        self.add(plane)

        formula = MathTex(r"q_i = \text{arctanh}(\beta_i) + q_{min}")
        formula.to_edge(UP)
        formula_bg = BackgroundRectangle(formula, color=BLACK, fill_opacity=0.8, buff=0.1)
        self.add(formula_bg, formula)

        # 2. Create Dots (Hits)
        num_dots = 40
        dots = VGroup()
        for _ in range(num_dots):
            pos = [random.uniform(-6, 6), random.uniform(-3, 2), 0]
            dot = Dot(point=pos, radius=0.08, color=PURPLE)
            # Assign a simulated beta value
            dot.beta_val = random.uniform(0.05, 0.2) 
            dots.add(dot)

        self.play(LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(d) for d in dots], lag_ratio=0.05))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Simulate Beta growth for "Condensation Points"
        num_clusters = 4
        # Choose random indices for condensation points
        cp_indices = random.sample(range(num_dots), num_clusters)
        
        grow_anims = []
        wells = VGroup()
        
        # Track CP objects
        condensation_points = []

        for idx in cp_indices:
            dot = dots[idx]
            dot.beta_val = random.uniform(0.8, 0.95)
            condensation_points.append(dot)
            
            # High beta dots turn yellow and grow
            grow_anims.append(dot.animate.set_color(YELLOW).scale(2.5))
            
            # Create "gravity well" effect using concentric circles
            well = VGroup(*[
                Circle(radius=r, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=1).set_opacity(0.6 - (r/1.5))
                for r in np.linspace(0.2, 1.2, 5)
            ])
            well.move_to(dot.get_center())
            wells.add(well)

        self.play(*grow_anims, run_time=1.5)
        self.play(Create(wells), run_time=1)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. Pulling dots toward centers
        pull_anims = []
        for i, dot in enumerate(dots):
            if i in cp_indices:
                continue
            
            # Find the nearest condensation point manually to avoid lambda scope issues
            best_dist = float('inf')
            nearest_cp = None
            for cp_obj in condensation_points:
                d = np.linalg.norm(cp_obj.get_center() - dot.get_center())
                if d < best_dist:
                    best_dist = d
                    nearest_cp = cp_obj
            
            # Only pull dots within a certain range
            if best_dist < 3.5:
                # Move towards center (75% of the way)
                direction = nearest_cp.get_center() - dot.get_center()
                target_pos = dot.get_center() + direction * 0.75
                
                # Blend color from PURPLE to YELLOW
                new_color = interpolate_color(PURPLE, YELLOW, 0.6)
                pull_anims.append(
                    dot.animate.move_to(target_pos).set_color(new_color)
                )

        # 5. Run the clustering animation
        if pull_anims:
            self.play(*pull_anims, run_time=2, rate_func=rate_functions.ease_in_sine)
        
        # Final highlight
        self.play(Indicate(formula))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class Concept02ObjectCondensation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Data and Parameters
        q_min = 0.5
        num_dots = 40
        
        # 2. Display the Formula
        formula = MathTex(r"q_i = \text{arctanh}(\beta_i) + q_{min}")
        formula.to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        
        # 3. Create a 2D plane of dots
        # We manually manage dots to track their beta values
        dots_group = VGroup()
        dots_data = []
        
        # Define some indices to be high-beta "condensation points"
        cp_indices = [8, 22, 35]
        
        for i in range(num_dots):
            # Random position in the frame
            pos = [np.random.uniform(-5, 5), np.random.uniform(-3, 1), 0]
            
            # Assign beta values
            if i in cp_indices:
                beta = np.random.uniform(0.85, 0.95)
            else:
                beta = np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.4)
            
            # Start with small purple dots
            dot = Dot(point=pos, radius=0.08, color=PURPLE)
            dots_group.add(dot)
            dots_data.append({
                "mobject": dot,
                "beta": beta,
                "is_cp": i in cp_indices
            })
            
        self.play(Create(dots_group))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # 4. Animate hits to charges (Beta-charge mechanism)
        # Dots grow in size and change color based on beta
        growth_anims = []
        for item in dots_data:
            beta = item["beta"]
            # Calculate charge q using the formula (clipped for stability)
            q = np.arctanh(np.clip(beta, 0.01, 0.99)) + q_min
            
            # Determine color: interpolate from PURPLE to YELLOW based on beta
            # Since interpolate_color was reported as undefined, we use a simple scale
            if beta > 0.8:
                target_color = YELLOW
            elif beta > 0.5:
                target_color = ORANGE
            else:
                target_color = PURPLE
                
            # Scale factor based on charge q
            target_scale = q * 1.2
            
            growth_anims.append(
                item["mobject"].animate.set_color(target_color).scale(target_scale)
            )
            
        self.play(*growth_anims, run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # 5. Create 'gravity wells' (potential functions)
        # Use concentric circles to represent the attractive potential
        wells = VGroup()
        cp_items = [d for d in dots_data if d["is_cp"]]
        for cp in cp_items:
            center = cp["mobject"].get_center()
            # Gradient circle effect using multiple concentric circles with low opacity
            well = VGroup(*[
                Circle(radius=r, stroke_width=1, color=YELLOW).set_opacity(0.4 - r/5)
                for r in [0.4, 0.8, 1.2, 1.6]
            ]).move_to(center)
            wells.add(well)
            
        self.play(FadeIn(wells, scale=0.8), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # 6. Clustering: Pull noise dots toward the condensation points
        # Neighboring low-beta dots slide toward the nearest gravity well
        pull_anims = []
        noise_items = [d for d in dots_data if not d["is_cp"]]
        for item in noise_items:
            start_pos = item["mobject"].get_center()
            # Find the center of the closest condensation point
            closest_cp_pos = min(
                [cp["mobject"].get_center() for cp in cp_items],
                key=lambda p: np.linalg.norm(p - start_pos)
            )
            
            # Calculate destination (move 70% toward the center)
            direction_vector = closest_cp_pos - start_pos
            destination = start_pos + direction_vector * 0.7
            
            # Use basic animate.move_to for maximum compatibility
            pull_anims.append(item["mobject"].animate.move_to(destination))
            
        self.play(*pull_anims, run_time=3, rate_func=bezier([0, 0, 1, 1]))
        
        # 7. Final Caption
        narration = Text("Condensation points act like gravity, pulling hits into particles.", font_size=24)
        narration.to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(narration))
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LearnedClusteringSpace(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Planes (Split Screen)
        # Using a simpler setup to ensure compatibility across Manim versions
        left_origin = 3.5 * LEFT
        right_origin = 3.5 * RIGHT
        
        left_plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            y_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            x_length=5,
            y_length=5,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}
        ).move_to(left_origin)
        
        right_plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            y_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            x_length=5,
            y_length=5,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}
        ).move_to(right_origin)

        left_title = Text("Physical Space", font_size=24).next_to(left_plane, UP)
        right_title = Text("Learned Space", font_size=24).next_to(right_plane, UP)

        self.play(
            Create(left_plane),
            Create(right_plane),
            Write(left_title),
            Write(right_title)
        )

        # 2. Create Overlapping Clusters for Physical Space
        # We generate random offsets in physical space where clusters overlap
        np.random.seed(42) # For reproducibility
        
        dots_a = VGroup()
        dots_b = VGroup()
        
        # Cluster A (Red) - centered slightly right
        for _ in range(20):
            p = left_plane.c2p(np.random.normal(0.2, 0.4), np.random.normal(0, 0.4), 0)
            dots_a.add(Dot(p, radius=0.06, color=RED))
            
        # Cluster B (Blue) - centered slightly left, overlapping A
        for _ in range(20):
            p = left_plane.c2p(np.random.normal(-0.2, 0.4), np.random.normal(0, 0.4), 0)
            dots_b.add(Dot(p, radius=0.06, color=BLUE))
        
        self.play(FadeIn(dots_a), FadeIn(dots_b))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Transformation to Learned Space
        # Define destination positions where they are clearly separated
        dots_a_animations = []
        for dot in dots_a:
            target_pos = right_plane.c2p(
                np.random.normal(1.5, 0.3), 
                np.random.normal(1.2, 0.3), 0
            )
            dots_a_animations.append(dot.animate.move_to(target_pos))

        dots_b_animations = []
        for dot in dots_b:
            target_pos = right_plane.c2p(
                np.random.normal(-1.5, 0.3), 
                np.random.normal(-1.2, 0.3), 0
            )
            dots_b_animations.append(dot.animate.move_to(target_pos))

        # Perform the "Unwarping" animation
        self.play(
            *dots_a_animations,
            *dots_b_animations,
            run_time=3,
            path_arc=0.3
        )

        # 4. Success indicators: Dashed circles
        # Using a circle with a dashed stroke to avoid DashedVMobject if it causes issues
        # In most Manim versions, DashedVMobject(Circle()) is the way to go.
        # If it fails, one can use many small arcs, but we'll use DashedVMobject as it is standard.
        circle_a = DashedVMobject(Circle(radius=0.9, color=RED).move_to(right_plane.c2p(1.5, 1.2, 0)))
        circle_b = DashedVMobject(Circle(radius=0.9, color=BLUE).move_to(right_plane.c2p(-1.5, -1.2, 0)))

        self.play(Create(circle_a), Create(circle_b))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Narration and Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"L_V = \frac{1}{N} \sum q_j \sum (\mathbb{1} \check{V}_k + (1-\mathbb{1}) \hat{V}_k)",
            font_size=28
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        
        # Using string "ITALIC" instead of constant to prevent undefined identifier errors
        voice_sub = Text(
            "Mapping hits to a multi-dimensional 'learned coordinate' space.",
            font_size=20,
            slant="ITALIC"
        ).next_to(formula, UP, buff=0.2)

        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(FadeIn(voice_sub))
        self.wait(3)

        # Fade out everything
        self.play(FadeOut(VGroup(self.mobjects)))
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LearnedClusteringAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Split-Screen View
        # Create two NumberPlanes for Physical and Learned spaces
        physical_plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-4, 4, 1],
            y_range=[-4, 4, 1],
            x_length=5.5,
            y_length=5.5,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)

        learned_plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-4, 4, 1],
            y_range=[-4, 4, 1],
            x_length=5.5,
            y_length=5.5,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5)

        p_title = Text("Physical Space", color=BLUE).scale(0.7).next_to(physical_plane, UP)
        l_title = Text("Learned Space", color=GREEN).scale(0.7).next_to(learned_plane, UP)

        self.add(physical_plane, learned_plane, p_title, l_title)

        # 2. Generate Clusters
        np.random.seed(42)
        num_dots = 40

        # Create points for Physical Space (High overlap)
        # Red cluster centered slightly right, Blue slightly left
        p_red_points = [
            physical_plane.c2p(np.random.normal(0.4, 0.8), np.random.normal(0.4, 0.8))
            for _ in range(num_dots)
        ]
        p_blue_points = [
            physical_plane.c2p(np.random.normal(-0.4, 0.8), np.random.normal(-0.4, 0.8))
            for _ in range(num_dots)
        ]

        # Create points for Learned Space (Distinct blobs)
        # Clusters are now far apart
        l_red_points = [
            learned_plane.c2p(np.random.normal(2.0, 0.4), np.random.normal(2.0, 0.4))
            for _ in range(num_dots)
        ]
        l_blue_points = [
            learned_plane.c2p(np.random.normal(-2.0, 0.4), np.random.normal(-2.0, 0.4))
            for _ in range(num_dots)
        ]

        dots_red = VGroup(*[Dot(p, color=RED, radius=0.06) for p in p_red_points])
        dots_blue = VGroup(*[Dot(p, color=BLUE, radius=0.06) for p in p_blue_points])

        # 3. Helper for Dashed Circles (Manual to avoid identifier errors)
        def get_dashed_circle(center, radius, color):
            dashes = VGroup()
            num_segments = 20
            for i in range(num_segments):
                dashes.add(Arc(
                    radius=radius,
                    start_angle=i * (TAU / num_segments),
                    angle=TAU / (num_segments * 2),
                    arc_center=center,
                    color=color,
                    stroke_width=2
                ))
            return dashes

        # 4. Animation Sequence
        # Show hits in physical space
        self.play(FadeIn(dots_red), FadeIn(dots_blue))
        self.wait(1)

        # Transform hits to learned space
        self.play(
            *[dots_red[i].animate.move_to(l_red_points[i]) for i in range(num_dots)],
            *[dots_blue[i].animate.move_to(l_blue_points[i]) for i in range(num_dots)],
            run_time=3,
            path_arc=0.3
        )

        # Draw classification circles
        circle_red = get_dashed_circle(learned_plane.c2p(2.0, 2.0), 1.0, RED)
        circle_blue = get_dashed_circle(learned_plane.c2p(-2.0, -2.0), 1.0, BLUE)
        
        self.play(Create(circle_red), Create(circle_blue))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 5. Text and Formula
        voice_text = Text(
            "Mapping hits to a multi-dimensional 'learned coordinate' space.",
            slant="ITALIC"
        ).scale(0.45).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.8)

        formula = MathTex(
            r"L_V = \frac{1}{N} \sum q_j \sum (\mathbb{1} \check{V}_k + (1-\mathbb{1}) \hat{V}_k)",
            color=YELLOW
        ).scale(0.7).next_to(voice_text, DOWN, buff=0.2)

        self.play(Write(voice_text))
        self.play(FadeIn(formula))

        self.wait(3)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LearnedClusteringSpace(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Split Screen Planes
        left_plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            y_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            x_length=5,
            y_length=5,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}
        ).shift(3.5 * LEFT)

        right_plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            y_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            x_length=5,
            y_length=5,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}
        ).shift(3.5 * RIGHT)

        left_label = Text("Physical Space", color=WHITE, font_size=24).next_to(left_plane, UP)
        right_label = Text("Learned Space", color=WHITE, font_size=24).next_to(right_plane, UP)

        # 2. Formula (Reference from Prompt)
        formula = MathTex(
            r"L_V = \frac{1}{N} \sum q_j \sum (\mathbb{1} \check{V}_k + (1-\mathbb{1}) \hat{V}_k)",
            color=WHITE, font_size=32
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)

        # 3. Points Generation
        np.random.seed(42)
        n_points = 35
        
        # Left side: Overlapping clusters
        center_left = left_plane.get_center()
        red_group_phys = VGroup()
        blue_group_phys = VGroup()
        
        for _ in range(n_points):
            # Centered close together to overlap
            pos_red = center_left + np.array([np.random.normal(0.3, 0.6), np.random.normal(0, 0.6), 0])
            pos_blue = center_left + np.array([np.random.normal(-0.3, 0.6), np.random.normal(0, 0.6), 0])
            red_group_phys.add(Dot(pos_red, color=RED, radius=0.05))
            blue_group_phys.add(Dot(pos_blue, color=BLUE, radius=0.05))

        # Right side: Distinct blobs
        center_red_learned = right_plane.get_center() + 1.2 * UP + 0.7 * RIGHT
        center_blue_learned = right_plane.get_center() + 1.2 * DOWN + 0.7 * LEFT
        
        red_group_learned = VGroup()
        blue_group_learned = VGroup()
        
        for _ in range(n_points):
            pos_red = center_red_learned + np.array([np.random.normal(0, 0.4), np.random.normal(0, 0.4), 0])
            pos_blue = center_blue_learned + np.array([np.random.normal(0, 0.4), np.random.normal(0, 0.4), 0])
            red_group_learned.add(Dot(pos_red, color=RED, radius=0.05))
            blue_group_learned.add(Dot(pos_blue, color=BLUE, radius=0.05))

        # 4. Dashed Circles (Created manually using Arc segments for max compatibility)
        def get_dashed_circle(center, radius, color):
            dashes = VGroup()
            n_segments = 20
            for i in range(n_segments):
                dashes.add(Arc(
                    radius=radius,
                    start_angle=i * (TAU / n_segments),
                    angle=TAU / (2 * n_segments),
                    arc_center=center,
                    color=color,
                    stroke_width=2
                ))
            return dashes

        circle_red = get_dashed_circle(center_red_learned, 1.0, RED)
        circle_blue = get_dashed_circle(center_blue_learned, 1.0, BLUE)

        # 5. Narration Text
        narration = Text(
            "Mapping hits from physical detector space to learned coordinate space...",
            font_size=20
        ).next_to(formula, UP, buff=0.3)

        # 6. Execution
        self.add(left_plane, right_plane, left_label, right_label, formula)
        self.play(FadeIn(red_group_phys), FadeIn(blue_group_phys))
        self.wait(1)
        
        self.play(Write(narration))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Transition to learned space
        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(red_group_phys, red_group_learned),
            ReplacementTransform(blue_group_phys, blue_group_learned),
            run_time=3,
            path_arc=0.5
        )
        
        # Final classification indicator
        self.play(Create(circle_red), Create(circle_blue))
        
        final_narration = Text(
            "Complex overlapping energy deposits are now easily separable.",
            font_size=20
        ).move_to(narration)
        
        self.play(FadeOut(narration), FadeIn(final_narration))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class EfficiencyVsOverlap(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Axes
        # Use a safe x-range and y-range
        ax = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 2.5, 0.5],
            y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.1],
            x_length=7,
            y_length=5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.5)

        x_label = ax.get_x_axis_label("Distance", edge=DOWN, direction=DOWN).scale(0.8)
        y_label = ax.get_y_axis_label("Efficiency", edge=LEFT, direction=LEFT).scale(0.8)
        
        ax_title = Text("Clustering Efficiency vs. Particle Distance", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)

        # 2. Plot Curves
        # ML model stays flat at 0.9. Use 'dist' as the variable name instead of 'x'
        ml_curve = ax.plot(lambda dist: 0.9, x_range=[0, 2.5], color=BLUE)
        ml_label = Text("ML Model", color=BLUE, font_size=24).next_to(ml_curve.get_end(), UP)

        # Standard algorithm drops sharply at low dist
        # Function: 0.9 * (1 - exp(-4*dist))
        std_curve_raw = ax.plot(lambda dist: 0.9 * (1.0 - np.exp(-4.0 * dist)), x_range=[0, 2.5], color=RED)
        
        # Wrapping in DashedVMobject. 
        # If DashedVMobject is problematic in some environments, we use the dash_pattern if available, 
        # but DashedVMobject is the community standard.
        std_curve = DashedVMobject(std_curve_raw, num_dashes=30)
        std_label = Text("Standard Algorithm", color=RED, font_size=24).next_to(std_curve.get_point_from_proportion(0.5), DOWN, buff=0.5)

        # 3. ValueTracker and Updaters for the Vertical Line and Particle Icons
        tracker = ValueTracker(2.4)
        
        # Vertical line indicating current distance
        v_line = always_redraw(lambda: 
            ax.get_vertical_line(
                ax.c2p(tracker.get_value(), 0.9), 
                color=YELLOW, 
                stroke_width=2
            )
        )

        # Particle Shower Icons - VGroup of dots representing cluster hits
        def get_shower_group(center_point, seed_val):
            np.random.seed(seed_val)
            dots = VGroup()
            for _ in range(12):
                offset = np.array([np.random.uniform(-0.2, 0.2), np.random.uniform(-0.2, 0.2), 0])
                dots.add(Dot(point=center_point + offset, radius=0.04, color=WHITE, fill_opacity=0.6))
            return dots

        icon_origin = LEFT * 3 + UP * 2
        
        # Redraw showers based on the tracker value
        shower_1 = always_redraw(lambda: 
            get_shower_group(icon_origin + LEFT * (tracker.get_value() * 0.4), 10)
        )
        shower_2 = always_redraw(lambda: 
            get_shower_group(icon_origin + RIGHT * (tracker.get_value() * 0.4), 20)
        )

        icon_header = Text("Particle Proximity", font_size=20).next_to(icon_origin, UP, buff=0.6)

        # 4. Animation Sequence
        self.add(ax, x_label, y_label, ax_title)
        self.play(Create(ml_curve), Write(ml_label))
        self.play(Create(std_curve), Write(std_label))
        self.wait(1)

        self.play(
            FadeIn(v_line),
            FadeIn(shower_1),
            FadeIn(shower_2),
            FadeIn(icon_header)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Move tracker to the left (distance closing). Using a lambda for linear rate to avoid identifier issues.
        self.play(
            tracker.animate.set_value(0.05),
            run_time=6,
            rate_func=lambda t: t
        )
        
        # Final descriptive text
        impact_text = Text("ML resolves overlaps", color=BLUE, font_size=28).move_to(ax.c2p(1.25, 0.5))
        self.play(Write(impact_text))
        
        self.wait(3)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class EfficiencyVsOverlap(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Axes
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            axis_config={"include_tip": True},
            x_length=9,
            y_length=5
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.5)

        x_label = Text("Distance between particles", font_size=20).next_to(axes.x_axis, DOWN)
        y_label = Text("Clustering Efficiency", font_size=20).rotate(90 * DEGREES).next_to(axes.y_axis, LEFT)

        # 2. Define Plotting Functions
        # Using 'val' instead of 'x' to avoid potential identifier conflicts
        def ml_efficiency_func(val):
            return 0.9

        def std_efficiency_func(val):
            # Drops to 0 as distance (val) approaches 0
            if val < 0.001:
                return 0
            return 0.9 * (1.0 - np.exp(-10.0 * val))

        # 3. Create Curves
        ml_curve = axes.plot(ml_efficiency_func, x_range=[0, 1], color=BLUE)
        
        # Manually create a dashed curve for the standard algorithm
        std_curve_base = axes.plot(std_efficiency_func, x_range=[0, 1], color=RED)
        std_curve = DashedVMobject(std_curve_base, num_dashes=40)

        ml_tag = Text("ML Model", color=BLUE, font_size=18).move_to(axes.c2p(0.5, 0.97))
        std_tag = Text("Standard Algorithm", color=RED, font_size=18).move_to(axes.c2p(0.7, 0.4))

        # 4. Interactive Elements (ValueTracker)
        # Starting at distance 1.0 (efficiency ~0.9 for both)
        dist_tracker = ValueTracker(1.0)

        # Vertical line following the tracker
        v_line = always_redraw(lambda: 
            axes.get_vertical_line(
                axes.c2p(dist_tracker.get_value(), 0.9),
                color=WHITE,
                stroke_width=2
            )
        )

        # Visual representation of particle showers merging
        def get_shower_icons(dist):
            center_y_pos = 2.8
            left_shower = VGroup(
                Dot(point=[-dist * 2, center_y_pos, 0], radius=0.1, color=YELLOW),
                Dot(point=[-dist * 2 + 0.1, center_y_pos + 0.1, 0], radius=0.03, color=YELLOW, fill_opacity=0.5)
            )
            right_shower = VGroup(
                Dot(point=[dist * 2, center_y_pos, 0], radius=0.1, color=YELLOW),
                Dot(point=[dist * 2 - 0.1, center_y_pos - 0.1, 0], radius=0.03, color=YELLOW, fill_opacity=0.5)
            )
            return VGroup(left_shower, right_shower)

        shower_icons = always_redraw(lambda: get_shower_icons(dist_tracker.get_value()))
        
        # Background for the icons
        shower_box = RoundedRectangle(height=1.0, width=5.0, corner_radius=0.1, color=GRAY).move_to(UP * 2.8)
        shower_label = Text("Particle Proximity", font_size=14, color=GRAY).next_to(shower_box, UP, buff=0.1)

        # 5. Build and Animate
        self.add(axes, x_label, y_label, ml_curve, std_curve, ml_tag, std_tag)
        self.add(shower_box, shower_label, v_line, shower_icons)
        
        # Narration text
        narration = Text(
            "ML maintains efficiency even as particles overlap.",
            font_size=20
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.2)
        
        self.play(Write(narration))
        
        # Animate the distance closing (moving to the left on the axis)
        self.play(
            dist_tracker.animate.set_value(0.01),
            run_time=8,
            rate_func=rate_functions.linear
        )
        self.wait(2)

{
  "id": "concept-03-iterative-clustering",
  "voice": "Standard clustering methods like K-Means often fail on sparse data, so this system uses an iterative approach. We start by finding the two most similar proposals in our matrix and use them to 'seed' a cluster. We then greedily pull in the next most similar unassigned proposal until we hit our target size. This ensures each group of nine proposals has the highest possible thematic cohesion, making it easier to find a single panel of reviewers who can cover the whole batch.",
  "goal": "Explain the step-by-step logic of the custom iterative clustering algorithm.",
  "remarks": "Generate a cloud of 27 Dot objects scattered randomly. Highlight the two dots with the shortest distance (highest similarity) using a bright ring. Animate a 'search' circle expanding from this pair; when it touches the next closest dot, that dot changes color to match the seed pair. Continue this until 9 dots are the same color. Repeat the process for two more clusters until all dots are grouped into three distinct colored sets.",
  "formula": null,
  "image_ref": null
}
```

Use the 'voice' for narration, 'goal' for the objective, and 'remarks' for the detailed animation blueprint.
generated_code:
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class IterativeClustering(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Configuration
        num_dots = 27
        dots_per_cluster = 9
        num_clusters = 3
        colors = [BLUE, GREEN, YELLOW]
        
        # Create a random cloud of 27 dots
        # Using a fixed seed for consistent layout
        np.random.seed(42)
        dots = VGroup()
        for _ in range(num_dots):
            pos = [np.random.uniform(-5, 5), np.random.uniform(-3, 3), 0]
            dots.add(Dot(point=pos, radius=0.1, color=WHITE))
        
        self.add(dots)
        self.wait(1)

        unassigned_dots = list(dots)
        
        for cluster_idx in range(num_clusters):
            cluster_color = colors[cluster_idx]
            current_cluster = []
            
            # 1. Find the two most similar (closest) unassigned dots to seed the cluster
            min_dist = float('inf')
            seed_pair = (None, None)
            
            if len(unassigned_dots) < 2:
                break
                
            for i in range(len(unassigned_dots)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(unassigned_dots)):
                    d1 = unassigned_dots[i]
                    d2 = unassigned_dots[j]
                    dist = np.linalg.norm(d1.get_center() - d2.get_center())
                    if dist < min_dist:
                        min_dist = dist
                        seed_pair = (d1, d2)
            
            # Highlight the seed pair
            d1, d2 = seed_pair
            unassigned_dots.remove(d1)
            unassigned_dots.remove(d2)
            current_cluster.extend([d1, d2])
            
            # Bright ring around the pair
            center_point = (d1.get_center() + d2.get_center()) / 2
            ring = Circle(radius=min_dist/2 + 0.2, color=cluster_color).move_to(center_point)
            
            self.play(
                Create(ring),
                d1.animate.set_color(cluster_color),
                d2.animate.set_color(cluster_color)
            )
            
            # 2. Greedily pull in the next most similar dots until target size (9)
            while len(current_cluster) < dots_per_cluster and len(unassigned_dots) > 0:
                best_dist = float('inf')
                next_dot = None
                
                # Find unassigned dot closest to ANY dot in the current cluster
                for d_un in unassigned_dots:
                    for d_cl in current_cluster:
                        dist = np.linalg.norm(d_un.get_center() - d_cl.get_center())
                        if dist < best_dist:
                            best_dist = dist
                            next_dot = d_un
                
                # Expand search circle to the next dot
                current_center = sum([d.get_center() for d in current_cluster]) / len(current_cluster)
                target_radius = np.linalg.norm(next_dot.get_center() - current_center)
                
                search_circle = Circle(radius=0.01, color=cluster_color, stroke_opacity=0.5).move_to(current_center)
                self.add(search_circle)
                
                self.play(
                    search_circle.animate.scale(target_radius / 0.01),
                    run_time=0.5,
                    rate_func=rate_functions.linear
                )
                
                # Update dot and cluster
                unassigned_dots.remove(next_dot)
                current_cluster.append(next_dot)
                
                self.play(
                    next_dot.animate.set_color(cluster_color),
                    ring.animate.move_to(current_center).set_width(target_radius * 2.2),
                    FadeOut(search_circle),
                    run_time=0.3
                )
                
            self.play(FadeOut(ring))
            self.wait(0.5)

        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class IterativeClusteringConcept(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Set seed for reproducible random layout
        np.random.seed(42)
        
        # 1. Generate a cloud of 27 Dot objects scattered randomly
        dot_positions = [
            np.array([np.random.uniform(-5.5, 5.5), np.random.uniform(-3, 2), 0])
            for _ in range(27)
        ]
        dots = VGroup(*[Dot(pos, color=GRAY_B) for pos in dot_positions])
        
        title = Text("Iterative Clustering Process", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add(title)
        self.play(Create(dots))
        self.wait(1)

        available_indices = list(range(27))
        cluster_colors = [RED, GREEN, BLUE]
        
        # 2. Process: 3 clusters of 9 dots each
        for cluster_idx in range(3):
            cluster_color = cluster_colors[cluster_idx]
            
            # Step A: Find the two most similar (shortest distance) unassigned dots to seed
            min_dist = float('inf')
            seed_pair = (None, None)
            
            for i in range(len(available_indices)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(available_indices)):
                    idx1 = available_indices[i]
                    idx2 = available_indices[j]
                    dist = np.linalg.norm(dots[idx1].get_center() - dots[idx2].get_center())
                    if dist < min_dist:
                        min_dist = dist
                        seed_pair = (idx1, idx2)
            
            s1, s2 = seed_pair
            current_cluster = [s1, s2]
            available_indices.remove(s1)
            available_indices.remove(s2)
            
            # Highlight the seed pair
            highlight1 = Circle(radius=0.2, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=2).move_to(dots[s1].get_center())
            highlight2 = Circle(radius=0.2, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=2).move_to(dots[s2].get_center())
            
            self.play(
                dots[s1].animate.set_color(cluster_color),
                dots[s2].animate.set_color(cluster_color),
                Create(highlight1), Create(highlight2),
                run_time=0.6
            )
            self.play(FadeOut(highlight1), FadeOut(highlight2), run_time=0.2)

            # Step B: Greedily pull in the next most similar unassigned proposals (7 more to reach 9)
            for _ in range(7):
                # We define cohesion by proximity to any member of the current cluster
                best_dist = float('inf')
                next_dot_idx = -1
                
                for candidate in available_indices:
                    for member in current_cluster:
                        d = np.linalg.norm(dots[candidate].get_center() - dots[member].get_center())
                        if d < best_dist:
                            best_dist = d
                            next_dot_idx = candidate
                
                # Animate search circle expanding from cluster centroid
                centroid = np.mean([dots[i].get_center() for i in current_cluster], axis=0)
                target_pos = dots[next_dot_idx].get_center()
                search_radius = np.linalg.norm(centroid - target_pos)
                
                search_circle = Circle(radius=0.1, color=WHITE, stroke_opacity=0.4).move_to(centroid)
                
                self.play(
                    search_circle.animate.set_width(search_radius * 2),
                    run_time=0.4,
                    rate_func=rate_functions.linear
                )
                self.play(
                    dots[next_dot_idx].animate.set_color(cluster_color),
                    FadeOut(search_circle),
                    run_time=0.3
                )
                
                current_cluster.append(next_dot_idx)
                available_indices.remove(next_dot_idx)
            
            self.wait(0.5)

        # Final layout check
        final_msg = Text("High Thematic Cohesion Achieved", font_size=24, color=YELLOW).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(final_msg))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import random

class ConceptMatrix(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Player Silhouettes (15 players)
        players = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                Circle(radius=0.1, color=GRAY, fill_opacity=1),
                Rectangle(width=0.2, height=0.2, color=GRAY, fill_opacity=1)
            ).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.05)
            for _ in range(15)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.15).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1)
        
        self.play(Create(players))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 2. Minutes Played Filter
        filter_rect = Rectangle(width=1, height=0.4, color=YELLOW).move_to(players[0])
        self.play(FadeIn(filter_rect))
        
        # Animate scan down to the 10th player
        self.play(filter_rect.animate.move_to(players[9]), run_time=1.5)
        
        top_10 = players[:10]
        others = players[10:]
        
        self.play(
            top_10.animate.set_color(BLUE),
            others.animate.set_opacity(0.2),
            FadeOut(filter_rect)
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Create Feature Matrix (10 rows x 15 columns)
        matrix_cells = VGroup()
        for r in range(10):
            row = VGroup(*[
                Square(side_length=0.3, stroke_width=1, color=WHITE)
                for _ in range(15)
            ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.05)
            matrix_cells.add(row)
        
        matrix_cells.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.05).shift(RIGHT * 2.5)
        
        # Stat Labels for Columns
        stat_names = ["PTS", "REB", "AST", "STL", "BLK", "TOV", "PF", "FG%", "FT%", "3P%", "+/-", "PER", "TS%", "USG%", "PIE"]
        stat_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(stat, font_size=10).next_to(matrix_cells[0][i], UP, buff=0.2).rotate(45*DEGREES)
            for i, stat in enumerate(stat_names)
        ])

        # 4. Animate Players sliding into Matrix Rows
        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(top_10.copy(), matrix_cells),
            Write(stat_labels),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Fill grid cells with "data" intensities
        fill_animations = []
        for row in matrix_cells:
            for cell in row:
                fill_animations.append(cell.animate.set_fill(BLUE, opacity=random.uniform(0.1, 1.0)))
        
        self.play(*fill_animations, run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Collapse 2D Grid into a single 1D Vector (150 elements)
        # Use MathTex for the formula reference
        formula = MathTex("n = m \\times p", font_size=36).to_corner(UL)
        formula_calc = MathTex("150 = 10 \\times 15", font_size=30).next_to(formula, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        
        self.play(Write(formula))
        
        # Flatten all cells into one group
        all_squares = VGroup(*[cell for row in matrix_cells for cell in row])
        
        self.play(
            all_squares.animate.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0).scale_to_fit_width(FRAME_WIDTH - 1).move_to(ORIGIN),
            FadeOut(stat_labels),
            FadeOut(players),
            FadeOut(others),
            Write(formula_calc),
            run_time=2
        )
        
        vector_text = Text("150-Element Team Vector", font_size=24).next_to(all_squares, UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(vector_text))
        
        # Highlight the final vector
        self.play(all_squares.animate.set_color(YELLOW), run_time=0.5)
        self.play(all_squares.animate.set_color(BLUE), run_time=0.5)
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class FeatureMatrix(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create player silhouettes (15 dots/circles)
        players = VGroup(*[
            Circle(radius=0.15, fill_opacity=0.6, color=BLUE) 
            for _ in range(15)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.2).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1)
        
        player_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(f"P{i+1}", font_size=14).next_to(players[i], LEFT, buff=0.2)
            for i in range(15)
        ])
        
        self.play(Create(players), FadeIn(player_labels))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 2. Minutes Played Filter
        filter_line = Line(LEFT, RIGHT, color=YELLOW).set_width(2)
        filter_line.next_to(players[0], UP, buff=0.1)
        filter_text = Text("Minutes Played Filter", font_size=20, color=YELLOW).next_to(filter_line, UP)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(filter_line), FadeIn(filter_text))
        # Animate filter passing over players down to the 10th player
        self.play(
            filter_line.animate.move_to(players[9].get_center() + DOWN*0.2),
            run_time=2
        )
        
        # Highlight top 10
        top_10 = players[:10]
        others = players[10:]
        others_labels = player_labels[10:]
        
        self.play(
            top_10.animate.set_color(GOLD).scale(1.2),
            FadeOut(others),
            FadeOut(others_labels),
            FadeOut(filter_line),
            FadeOut(filter_text)
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Create 10x15 Matrix
        matrix_group = VGroup()
        rows = 10
        cols = 15
        cell_size = 0.35
        
        # Colors for interpolation
        c1 = "#0000FF" # Pure Blue
        c2 = "#ADD8E6" # Light Blue
        
        for r in range(rows):
            for c in range(cols):
                val = np.random.random()
                color = interpolate_color(color_from=c1, color_to=c2, alpha=val)
                sq = Square(side_length=cell_size, fill_color=color, fill_opacity=0.8, stroke_width=1)
                matrix_group.add(sq)
        
        matrix_group.arrange_in_grid(rows=rows, cols=cols, buff=0.05).shift(RIGHT * 2)
        
        # 4. Slide top 10 players into rows
        # We transform the player dots into the first column of the matrix
        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(top_10, matrix_group[::cols]),
            player_labels[:10].animate.next_to(matrix_group, LEFT, buff=0.2)
        )
        self.play(FadeIn(matrix_group[1:])) # Fade in the rest of the cells
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 5. Column Labels
        stat_names = ["PTS", "REB", "AST", "STL", "BLK", "TOV", "PF", "MIN", "FGM", "FGA", "3PM", "3PA", "FTM", "FTA", "PIE"]
        col_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(stat_names[i], font_size=12).next_to(matrix_group[i], UP, buff=0.2)
            for i in range(cols)
        ])
        self.play(FadeIn(col_labels))
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Formula
        formula = MathTex("n = m \\times p", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Collapse into 1D Vector (150 elements)
        # Create a single long horizontal vector
        vector_cells = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.08, fill_color=cell.get_fill_color(), fill_opacity=0.8, stroke_width=0.1)
            for cell in matrix_group
        ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.01).move_to(DOWN * 2)
        
        # Ensure it fits the frame
        vector_cells.set_width(12)

        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(matrix_group, vector_cells),
            FadeOut(col_labels),
            FadeOut(player_labels[:10]),
            formula.animate.scale(1.2).next_to(vector_cells, UP, buff=0.5)
        )
        
        label_150 = Text("150-Element Feature Vector", font_size=20).next_to(vector_cells, DOWN, buff=0.3)
        self.play(Write(label_150))
        self.wait(2)
