from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptMatrix(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create a silhouette representation of 15 basketball players
        # Using simple shapes to represent silhouettes
        players = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                Circle(radius=0.12, color=WHITE),
                Triangle(color=WHITE).scale(0.15).next_to(Circle(radius=0.12), DOWN, buff=0.02)
            )
            for _ in range(15)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.1).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        
        player_label = Text("Roster (15 Players)", font_size=20).next_to(players, UP)
        
        self.play(Create(players), Write(player_label))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 2. Animate a 'minutes played' filter highlighting the top 10
        filter_rect = Rectangle(width=0.8, height=3.8, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=3)
        filter_rect.move_to(players[:10])
        
        filter_text = Text("Top 10 by Minutes", font_size=16, color=YELLOW).next_to(filter_rect, RIGHT)

        self.play(Create(filter_rect), Write(filter_text))
        self.play(players[10:].animate.set_opacity(0.2))
        self.wait(1)
        self.play(FadeOut(filter_text))

        # 3. Create a 10x15 grid (10 players x 15 stats)
        # Rows = 10 (Players), Columns = 15 (Stats)
        grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.35, stroke_width=1, fill_opacity=0).set_color(GRAY_A)
            for _ in range(150)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=10, columns=15, buff=0.05).shift(RIGHT * 2.5)

        # Labels for stats above columns
        stat_names = ["PTS", "REB", "AST", "STL", "BLK", "TOV", "FG%", "FT%", "3P%", "OR", "DR", "PF", "PM", "USG", "PIE"]
        labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(name, font_size=10).next_to(grid[i], UP, buff=0.1).rotate(45*DEGREES)
            for i in range(15)
        ])

        # 4. Animate top 10 players sliding into the rows of the grid
        self.play(
            players[:10].animate.scale(0.8).next_to(grid, LEFT, buff=0.2),
            FadeIn(grid),
            Write(labels),
            filter_rect.animate.fade(1)
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 5. Fill the grid cells with varying intensities of color (normalized data)
        # We'll use a loop but wrap it in one play call for performance
        fill_animations = []
        for cell in grid:
            val = np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.9)
            fill_animations.append(cell.animate.set_fill(BLUE_C, opacity=val).set_stroke(width=0.5))
        
        self.play(LaggedStart(*fill_animations, lagged_ratio=0.005, run_time=2))
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Formula Reference
        formula = MathTex("n = m \\times p", color=YELLOW).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Collapse this 2D grid into a single long horizontal vector
        # Capture current width for scaling to fit screen
        target_width = 12 # Approximation of frame width
        
        vector_group = grid.copy()
        
        self.play(
            FadeOut(labels),
            FadeOut(players),
            FadeOut(player_label),
            grid.animate.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0).scale_to_fit_width(target_width).move_to(ORIGIN)
        )
        
        vector_bracket = Brace(grid, DOWN, buff=0.2)
        vector_text = Text("Feature Vector (150 Dimensions)", font_size=24).next_to(vector_bracket, DOWN)
        
        self.play(Create(vector_bracket), Write(vector_text))
        self.wait(2)

        # Final emphasis on the model input
        model_box = Rectangle(width=2, height=1.5, color=GREEN).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        model_text = Text("ML Model", font_size=20).move_to(model_box)
        
        self.play(
            grid.animate.scale(0.2).next_to(model_box, LEFT, buff=1),
            vector_bracket.animate.scale(0.2).next_to(model_box, LEFT, buff=1),

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class RegularizationTradeoff(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Main Plot Setup (MSE vs Dimensions)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 11, 1],
            y_range=[0, 12, 2],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5).shift(DOWN * 0.5)

        x_label = Tex("Number of Players", font_size=24).next_to(axes.x_axis, DOWN)
        y_label = Tex("Mean Squared Error", font_size=24).rotate(90 * DEGREES).next_to(axes.y_axis, LEFT)
        axes_labels = VGroup(x_label, y_label)

        # KNN Curve: Overfitting visualization (dips then rises sharply)
        def knn_fn(t):
            return 0.4 * (t - 3.5)**2 + 1.5
        
        knn_curve = axes.plot(
            knn_fn,
            x_range=[1, 10],
            color=RED
        )
        knn_tag = Tex("KNN", color=RED, font_size=24).next_to(knn_curve.get_end(), UR, buff=0.1)

        # Lasso/Ridge Curve: Regularization visualization (steady decline)
        def lasso_fn(t):
            return 9 * np.exp(-0.2 * t) + 0.5
            
        lasso_curve = axes.plot(
            lasso_fn,
            x_range=[1, 10],
            color=GREEN
        )
        lasso_tag = Tex("Lasso / Ridge", color=GREEN, font_size=24).next_to(lasso_curve.get_end(), DR, buff=0.1)

        # 2. Title and Formula
        title = Text("Regularization Tradeoff", font_size=32).to_edge(UP, buff=0.3)
        lasso_formula = MathTex(
            r"J(\theta) = \|y - X\theta\|^2 + \lambda \|\theta\|_1",
            font_size=36
        ).next_to(title, DOWN, buff=0.3)

        # 3. Coefficient Shrinkage Visualization (Right Side)
        num_coeffs = 100 
        coeff_axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, num_coeffs, 10],
            y_range=[0, 5, 1],
            x_length=5,
            y_length=3,
            tips=False,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5).shift(DOWN * 0.5)
        
        coeff_title = Tex("Coefficients $\\theta$", font_size=24).next_to(coeff_axes, UP)
        
        np.random.seed(42)
        initial_heights = np.random.uniform(0.1, 4.0, num_coeffs)
        
        lambda_val = ValueTracker(0)

        # Bars representing coefficients
        bars = VGroup()
        for i in range(num_coeffs):
            h_init = initial_heights[i]
            # Use line to represent the coefficient magnitude
            bar = Line(
                coeff_axes.c2p(i, 0),
                coeff_axes.c2p(i, h_init),
                color=GREEN,
                stroke_width=1.5
            )
            bar.initial_h = h_init
            bar.idx = i
            bars.add(bar)

        # Updater to simulate Lasso shrinkage: coeff = max(0, original - lambda)
        def update_coeff_bars(m):
            current_lambda = lambda_val.get_value()
            for b in m:
                new_h = max(0, b.initial_h - current_lambda)
                b.put_start_and_end_on(
                    coeff_axes.c2p(b.idx, 0),
                    coeff_axes.c2p(b.idx, new_h)
                )
                if new_h <= 0:
                    b.set_stroke(opacity=0)
                else:
                    b.set_stroke(opacity=0.7)

        bars.add_updater(update_coeff_bars)

        lambda_display = always_redraw(lambda: 
            MathTex(f"\\lambda = {lambda_val.get_value():.2f}", font_size=30, color=YELLOW)
            .next_to(coeff_axes, DOWN)
        )

        # 4. Rendering
        self.add(axes, axes_labels, title, lasso_formula)
        self.play(Create(knn_curve), Write(knn_tag))
        self.play(Create(lasso_curve), Write(lasso_tag))
        self.wait(0.5)

        self.play(
            Create(coeff_axes), 
            Write(coeff_title),
            Create(bars),
            Write(lambda_display)
        )
        
        # Animate lambda increasing to show coefficients vanishing (Lasso feature selection)
        self.play(
            lambda_val.animate.set_value(3.5),
            run_time=8,
            rate_func=lambda t: t # Linear transition
        )
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Axes
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 10, 1],
            y_range=[0, 10, 2],
            x_length=7,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True},
            tips=False
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=1.0).shift(LEFT * 2)

        x_label = axes.get_x_axis_label("Num Players", edge=DOWN, direction=DOWN, buff=0.2).scale(0.6)
        y_label = axes.get_y_axis_label("MSE", edge=LEFT, direction=LEFT, buff=0.2).scale(0.6).rotate(90 * DEGREES)
        
        # 2. Plot Curves using named functions to avoid static analysis issues
        def knn_path(t):
            return 0.3 * (t - 3)**2 + 1.5
            
        def lasso_path(t):
            return 7 / (np.sqrt(t) + 0.5)

        knn_curve = axes.plot(knn_path, x_range=[1, 8], color=RED)
        knn_label = Tex("KNN", color=RED).scale(0.6).next_to(knn_curve.get_end(), UR, buff=0.1)

        lasso_curve = axes.plot(lasso_path, x_range=[1, 9.5], color=GREEN)
        lasso_label = Tex("Lasso / Ridge", color=GREEN).scale(0.6).next_to(lasso_curve.get_end(), RIGHT, buff=0.1)

        self.play(Create(axes), Write(x_label), Write(y_label))
        self.play(Create(knn_curve), Write(knn_label))
        self.play(Create(lasso_curve), Write(lasso_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Lasso Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"J(\theta) = \|y - X\theta\|^2 + \lambda \|\theta\|_1",
            color=WHITE
        ).scale(0.9).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1).shift(UP * 2)
        
        formula_box = SurroundingRectangle(formula, color=YELLOW, buff=0.2)
        
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(Create(formula_box))
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Coefficients Bar Chart (Simulating 150 weights)
        lambda_tracker = ValueTracker(0)
        
        np.random.seed(42)
        initial_weights = np.random.uniform(0.1, 2.5, 150)
        
        bars = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(
                width=0.03, 
                height=w, 
                fill_color=BLUE, 
                fill_opacity=0.8, 
                stroke_width=0
            ) for w in initial_weights
        ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.01).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)

        bar_label = Tex("Coefficients", color=WHITE).scale(0.7).next_to(bars, UP, buff=0.2)
        
        lambda_display = MathTex(r"\lambda = ", color=YELLOW).scale(0.8).next_to(formula_box, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        lambda_value = DecimalNumber(0, color=YELLOW).scale(0.8).next_to(lambda_display, RIGHT)
        
        def update_decimal(mob):
            mob.set_value(lambda_tracker.get_value())
        lambda_value.add_updater(update_decimal)

        self.play(Create(bars), Write(bar_label))
        self.play(Write(lambda_display), Write(lambda_value))

        # 5. Animate the shrinking and vanishing coefficients (Lasso penalty)
        def update_bars(m):
            current_l = lambda_tracker.get_value()
            for i, bar in enumerate(m):
                # Lasso soft-thresholding logic
                val = initial_weights[i] - current_l
                new_h = val if val > 0.001 else 0.001
                bar.set_height(new_h, stretch=True, about_edge=DOWN)
                if new_h <= 0.01:
                    bar.set_opacity(0)
                else:
                    bar.set_opacity(0.8)

        bars.add_updater(update_bars)
        
        self.play(
            lambda_tracker.animate.set_value(2.0),
            run_time=5,
            rate_func=rate_functions.linear
        )
        self.wait(2)

        # Fade out everything
        self.play(FadeOut(Group(*self.mobjects)))

generated_code:
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Axes
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 11, 1],
            y_range=[0, 5, 1],
            x_length=7,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True},
            tips=False
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        
        labels = axes.get_axis_labels(
            x_label="Number of Players",
            y_label="MSE"
        )

        # 2. Data for curves
        x_vals = np.arange(1, 11)
        # KNN: dipping then rising sharply (overfitting)
        y_knn = [3.5, 2.0, 1.5, 1.8, 2.5, 3.5, 5.0, 7.0, 10.0, 14.0] 
        # Lasso: steady decline as it ignores noise
        y_lasso = [3.8, 3.2, 2.8, 2.5, 2.3, 2.1, 2.0, 1.9, 1.85, 1.8]

        # Manually constructing graphs to avoid potential version-specific plotting issues
        knn_points = [axes.c2p(x, min(y, 4.8)) for x, y in zip(x_vals, y_knn)]
        knn_graph = VMobject(color=RED, stroke_width=4).set_points_as_corners(knn_points)
        knn_label = Text("KNN", color=RED, font_size=24).next_to(knn_graph, UR, buff=-0.5)

        lasso_points = [axes.c2p(x, y) for x, y in zip(x_vals, y_lasso)]
        lasso_graph = VMobject(color=GREEN, stroke_width=4).set_points_as_corners(lasso_points)
        lasso_label = Text("Lasso/Ridge", color=GREEN, font_size=24).next_to(lasso_graph, RIGHT, buff=0.2)

        self.play(Create(axes), Write(labels))
        self.play(Create(knn_graph), Write(knn_label))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Create(lasso_graph), Write(lasso_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Formula and Feature Selection visualization
        # Shift the plot to make room for the penalty demo
        self.play(
            VGroup(axes, labels, knn_graph, knn_label, lasso_graph, lasso_label).animate.scale(0.7).to_edge(UL)
        )

        formula = MathTex(
            r"J(\theta) = \|y - X\theta\|^2 + \lambda \|\theta\|_1",
            color=WHITE
        ).to_edge(UP).shift(RIGHT * 2)
        
        self.play(Write(formula))
        
        # 4. Coefficients Bar Chart to visualize Shrinkage
        num_coeffs = 40 
        np.random.seed(42)
        initial_weights = np.random.uniform(-1.5, 1.5, num_coeffs)
        
        lambda_tracker = ValueTracker(0)
        
        def get_bars():
            l_val = lambda_tracker.get_value()
            bars = VGroup()
            for i in range(num_coeffs):
                w = initial_weights[i]
                # Lasso penalty effect: soft thresholding
                # Coefficient shrinks towards zero and stays at zero
                new_h = np.sign(w) * max(0, np.abs(w) - l_val)
                bar = Line(
                    start=[i * 0.12, 0, 0],
                    end=[i * 0.12, new_h, 0],
                    stroke_width=3,
                    color=BLUE if abs(new_h) > 0.001 else GRAY
                )
                bars.add(bar)
            # Center the bar group and position it on the right
            return bars.center().to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.0).shift(RIGHT * 2.5)

        coeff_bars = always_redraw(get_bars)
        coeff_title = Text("Model Coefficients", font_size=20).next_to(coeff_bars, UP, buff=0.5)

        self.play(Create(coeff_bars), Write(coeff_title))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Animate Lambda Increase and show coefficients vanishing
        lambda_display_label = MathTex(r"\lambda = ", font_size=36).next_to(formula, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        lambda_val_text = always_redraw(lambda: 
            DecimalNumber(lambda_tracker.get_value(), num_decimal_places=2)
            .next_to(lambda_display_label, RIGHT)
        )

        self.play(Write(lambda_display_label), Write(lambda_val_text))
        
        # Use rate_functions.linear to ensure identifier exists in all Manim environments
        self.play(
            lambda_tracker.animate.set_value(1.6), 
            run_time=5, 
            rate_func=rate_functions.linear
        )
        self.wait(2)

        # Cleanup
        self.play(
            FadeOut(axes, labels, knn_graph, knn_label, lasso_graph, lasso_label),
            FadeOut(formula, lambda_display_label, lambda_val_text, coeff_bars, coeff_title)
        )
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *

class PlayerDepthImportance(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Voiceover and Data
        voice_text = (
            "The models reveal a hidden hierarchy in team success. While the top player's "
            "Impact Estimate is the strongest predictor, the 'star' alone isn't enough. "
            "Interestingly, the statistics of the fifth and eighth players carry surprising weight. "
            "This suggests that a team's floor is set by the quality of its fifth starter and its "
            "final rotation player, proving that depth is just as measurable as stardom."
        )
        
        # Coefficients representing importance (1st, 2nd, 5th, and 8th are highlighted)
        coeffs = [0.85, 0.65, 0.22, 0.18, 0.52, 0.15, 0.12, 0.42, 0.08, 0.05]
        
        # 2. Create Player Icons (Circles with Rank Labels)
        players = VGroup()
        for i in range(1, 11):
            circle = Circle(radius=0.3, color=WHITE, fill_opacity=0.2)
            label = Text(str(i), font_size=20).move_to(circle.get_center())
            player_unit = VGroup(circle, label)
            players.add(player_unit)
        
        players.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.4).to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.8)

        # 3. Create Bars (Vertical bars for Average Coefficient)
        bars = VGroup()
        for i, val in enumerate(coeffs):
            h = val * 4.5 + 0.1  # Scaling factor for visual height
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.4, 
                height=h, 
                fill_opacity=0.6, 
                stroke_width=1, 
                color=WHITE
            )
            # Align the bottom of the bar to the top of the player icon
            bar.next_to(players[i], UP, buff=0.1, aligned_edge=DOWN)
            bars.add(bar)

        # 4. Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\text{Wins} \approx \beta_1(\text{1st\_PIE}) + \beta_5(\text{5th\_PIE}) + \dots + \beta_8(\text{8th\_PIE})",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.7)

        # 5. Narrative Text Setup
        narr_label = Text(voice_text, font_size=17, line_spacing=1.5)
        # Manually create a background rectangle for the text
        narr_bg = Rectangle(
            width=13.5, 
            height=1.8, 
            fill_color=BLACK, 
            fill_opacity=0.8, 
            stroke_width=0
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0)
        narr_label.move_to(narr_bg.get_center())

        # 6. Animation Execution
        # Step 1: Show players
        self.play(Create(players), run_time=1.2)
        
        # Step 2: Bars grow and Formula appears
        # We use a simple Create animation which looks like growing for vertical rectangles
        self.play(
            *[Create(bar) for bar in bars],
            Write(formula),
            run_time=2,
            rate_func=smooth
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Step 3: Highlight Stars (1st and 2nd)
        star_bars = VGroup(bars[0], bars[1])
        star_icons = VGroup(players[0][0], players[1][0]) # The circles
        self.play(
            star_bars.animate.set_color(GOLD).set_fill(GOLD, opacity=0.9),
            star_icons.animate.set_color(GOLD),
            run_time=1
        )
        # Pulse effect using scale
        self.play(star_bars.animate.scale(1.1, about_edge=DOWN), run_time=0.4)
        self.play(star_bars.animate.scale(1/1.1, about_edge=DOWN), run_time=0.4)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Step 4: Highlight Depth (5th and 8th)
        depth_bars = VGroup(bars[4], bars[7])
        depth_icons = VGroup(players[4][0], players[7][0])
        self.play(
            depth_bars.animate.set_color(BLUE).set_fill(BLUE, opacity=0.9),
            depth_icons.animate.set_color(BLUE),
            run_time=1
        )
        
        # Pulsing blue light (2 pulses)
        for _ in range(2):
            self.play(depth_bars.animate.scale(1.15, about_edge=DOWN), run_time=0.35)
            self.play(depth_bars.animate.scale(1/1.15, about_edge=DOWN), run_time=0.35)

        # Step 5: Narration display
        self.play(FadeIn(narr_bg), Write(narr_label), run_time=6)
        
        # Emphasis on specific formula parts
        self.play(
            formula[0][6:16].animate.set_color(GOLD),  # beta_1(1st_PIE)
            formula[0][17:27].animate.set_color(BLUE), # beta_5(5th_PIE)
            formula[0][31:41].animate.set_color(BLUE), # beta_8(8th_PIE)
            run_time=2
        )
        
        self.wait(4)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    scene = PlayerDepthImportance()
    scene.render()

from manim import *

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Header Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\text{Wins} \approx \beta_1(\text{1st\_PIE}) + \beta_5(\text{5th\_PIE}) + \dots + \beta_8(\text{8th\_PIE})",
            font_size=36
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)

        # 2. Create Player Icons and Labels
        player_icons = VGroup()
        labels = VGroup()
        for i in range(1, 11):
            # Create a simple representation of a basketball player icon (circle + cross)
            circ = Circle(radius=0.25, color=WHITE)
            l1 = Line(circ.get_top(), circ.get_bottom(), stroke_width=1)
            l2 = Line(circ.get_left(), circ.get_right(), stroke_width=1)
            icon = VGroup(circ, l1, l2)
            
            # Create labels (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.)
            suffix = "th"
            if i == 1: suffix = "st"
            elif i == 2: suffix = "nd"
            elif i == 3: suffix = "rd"
            label = Text(f"{i}{suffix}", font_size=18)
            
            player_icons.add(icon)
            labels.add(label)

        player_icons.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.4).shift(DOWN * 1)
        for i in range(10):
            labels[i].next_to(player_icons[i], DOWN, buff=0.2)

        # 3. Create Coefficient Bars
        # Heights reflecting the prompt's finding (1st, 2nd strong, 5th and 8th surprisingly high)
        heights = [3.5, 3.0, 1.2, 1.0, 2.5, 1.1, 0.8, 2.0, 0.6, 0.4]
        bars = VGroup()
        for i, h in enumerate(heights):
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.4, 
                height=h, 
                fill_opacity=0.6, 
                stroke_width=1, 
                color=WHITE
            )
            bar.next_to(player_icons[i], UP, buff=0)
            bars.add(bar)

        # 4. Animation Sequence
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(0.3)
        self.play(
            Create(player_icons),
            FadeIn(labels, shift=UP),
            run_time=1.5
        )

        # Animate bars growing (using FadeIn with shift as a fallback for GrowFromEdge)
        self.play(
            LaggedStart(*[
                FadeIn(bar, shift=UP) for bar in bars
            ], lag_ratio=0.1),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Highlight 1st and 2nd (Stars) with Gold
        self.play(
            bars[0].animate.set_color(GOLD).set_fill(GOLD, opacity=0.8),
            bars[1].animate.set_color(GOLD).set_fill(GOLD, opacity=0.8),
        )
        self.play(Indicate(bars[0], color=GOLD), Indicate(bars[1], color=GOLD))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Highlight 5th and 8th (Crucial Depth) with Pulsing Blue
        self.play(
            bars[4].animate.set_color(BLUE).set_fill(BLUE, opacity=0.8),
            bars[7].animate.set_color(BLUE).set_fill(BLUE, opacity=0.8),
        )
        # Pulse effect
        for _ in range(2):
            self.play(
                Indicate(bars[4], scale_factor=1.2, color=BLUE),
                Indicate(bars[7], scale_factor=1.2, color=BLUE),
                run_time=0.8
            )

        # 5. Narration Text
        narration_str = "A team's floor is set by its 5th starter and final rotation player."
        narration = Text(narration_str, font_size=22, color=GREEN).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(Write(narration))
        self.wait(3)
